content.text:
nstalling the CLI and enabling Entire in a Git repository.

1
Install the CLI
Install Entire in your terminal.


curl
curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

2
Log in
Link the CLI to your Entire account.

entire login

3
Enable checkpoints
From any Git repository, enable Entire so every push can publish checkpoints.

entire enable

4
Code and push
Work with your coding agents as usual. When you push, Entire links captured sessions to your commits and your first checkpoint will appear here.

i'm trying to get entire to send some checkpoints to the console pleaes help me do this
I'll help you get Entire publishing checkpoints. Let me first check the current state of your setup.

content.text: Key finding: **no Entire git hook is installed** in `.git/hooks/` (the grep for "entire" matched nothing). Let me confirm that cleanly, since a missing hook would explain why you have 0 checkpoints.

input.command: cd /Users/elvis/Documents/ai/gtm-sdk; echo "=== files in .git/hooks (non-sample) ==="; find .git/hooks -type f ! -name '*.sample' 2>/dev/null; echo "=== post-commit hook? ==="; test -f .git/hooks/post-commit && echo "post-commit EXISTS" || echo "post-commit MISSING"; echo "=== core.hooksPath ==="; git config --get core.hooksPath || echo "(not set — using default .git/hooks)"
input.description: Cleanly enumerate installed git hooks and hooksPath

result.output:
=== files in .git/hooks (non-sample) ===
=== post-commit hook? ===
pyenv: pytest: command not found

The `pytest' command exists in these Python versions:
  3.14.3

Note: See 'pyenv help global' for tips on allowing both
      python2 and python3 to be found.

=== core.hooksPath ===
/Users/elvis/.cache/trunk/repos/cd9513b395b063d7764d817334290c23/git-hooks

content.text: Found the root cause. `core.hooksPath` is redirected to **trunk's** hooks directory (`~/.cache/trunk/.../git-hooks`), which **overrides `.git/hooks/`**. Entire installs its checkpoint hook into `.git/hooks/`, but git never looks there because trunk took over the hook path. So no hook fires on commit → 0 checkpoints. Let me confirm whether trunk's hook chains to Entire.

input.command: HOOKS=/Users/elvis/.cache/trunk/repos/cd9513b395b063d7764d817334290c23/git-hooks; echo "=== trunk hooks dir ==="; ls -la "$HOOKS" 2>&1; echo; echo "=== does trunk post-commit chain to entire? ==="; grep -ril entire "$HOOKS" 2>/dev/null || echo "NO reference to entire in trunk hooks"

content.text:
Excellent — this is the real state, and it's actually wired correctly. Entire detected that **trunk** owns `core.hooksPath` and chained itself into trunk's hooks rather than fighting it:

- `post-commit` (rewritten today 16:26) → runs trunk's logic **and** Entire
- `post-commit.pre-entire` → backup symlink to the original trunk-only hook
- Same `.pre-entire` backup pattern for `pre-push`

Let me confirm the rewritten `post-commit` actually invokes `entire`:

input.command: HOOKS=/Users/elvis/.cache/trunk/repos/cd9513b395b063d7764d817334290c23/git-hooks; echo "=== post-commit ==="; cat "$HOOKS/post-commit"; echo; echo "=== pre-push ==="; cat "$HOOKS/pre-push"
input.description: Read the entire-chained post-commit and pre-push hooks

result.output:
=== post-commit ===
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Post-commit hook: condense session data if commit has Entire-Checkpoint trailer
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git post-commit 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi
# Chain: run pre-existing hook
_entire_hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")"
if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" "$@"
fi

=== pre-push ===
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Pre-push hook: push session logs alongside user's push
# $1 is the remote name (e.g., "origin")
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git pre-push "$1"; else :; fi
# Chain: run pre-existing hook
_entire_hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")"
if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/pre-push.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/pre-push.pre-entire" "$@"
fi

content.text: The mechanism is now crystal clear. The key line: **"condense session data if commit has `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer"**. In `manual-commit` mode, a commit only becomes a checkpoint if its message carries an `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer — and the chained `prepare-commit-msg` hook is what should inject that trailer during an active session. Then `pre-push` ships the session logs to the console. Let me confirm the trailer is auto-injected and check the mode options.

input.command: HOOKS=/Users/elvis/.cache/trunk/repos/cd9513b395b063d7764d817334290c23/git-hooks; echo "=== prepare-commit-msg ==="; cat "$HOOKS/prepare-commit-msg"; echo; echo "=== commit-msg ==="; cat "$HOOKS/commit-msg"
input.description: Read prepare-commit-msg and commit-msg hooks to see trailer injection

result.output:
=== prepare-commit-msg ===
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git prepare-commit-msg "$1" "$2" 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi

=== commit-msg ===
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Commit-msg hook: strip trailer if no user content (allows aborting empty commits)
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git commit-msg "$1" || true; else printf '%s\n' '[entire] Entire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH. Skipping Entire Git hook; continuing. Installation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods' >&2 || :; fi

input.command: echo "=== enable --help (modes) ==="; entire enable --help 2>&1 | head -40; echo "=== hooks git --help ==="; entire hooks git --help 2>&1 | head -30
input.description: Show enable modes and git hooks subcommands

result.output:
=== enable --help (modes) ===
Enable Entire with session tracking for your AI agent workflows.

If Entire is not yet configured, this runs the full configuration flow.
If Entire is already configured but disabled, this re-enables it.

If the current directory is not a git repository, Entire can initialize one
for you and (optionally) create a matching GitHub repository via the gh CLI.

Usage:
  entire enable [flags]

Flags:
      --absolute-git-hook-path          Embed full binary path in git hooks (for GUI git clients that don't source shell profiles)
      --agent string                    Agent to set up hooks for (e.g., claude-code, codex, copilot-cli, cursor, factoryai-droid, gemini, opencode, pi; external agents on $PATH are also available). Enables non-interactive mode.
      --checkpoint-remote string        Checkpoint remote in provider:owner/repo format (e.g., github:org/checkpoints-repo)
  -f, --force                           Force reinstall hooks (removes existing Entire hooks first)
  -h, --help                            help for enable
      --init-repo                       If not a git repo, initialize one non-interactively
      --initial-commit-message string   Commit message for the initial commit when bootstrapping a new repo
      --local                           Write settings to .entire/settings.local.json instead of .entire/settings.json
      --no-github                       Initialize local git repo only; skip creating a GitHub remote
      --no-init-repo                    If not a git repo, exit instead of prompting to initialize one
      --project                         Write settings to .entire/settings.json even if it already exists
      --repo-name string                GitHub repository name for the new repo (used when bootstrapping)
      --repo-owner string               GitHub user or organization login for the new repo
      --repo-visibility string          GitHub repository visibility: public, private, or internal
      --search-skill                    Install the optional Entire search skill for selected agent(s)
      --skip-initial-commit             Don't create the initial commit when bootstrapping a new repo
      --skip-push-sessions              Disable automatic pushing of session logs on git push
      --telemetry                       Enable anonymous usage analytics (default true)
  -y, --yes                             Accept all defaults without prompting (in a non-repo directory: init git, create private GitHub repo, commit; then enable all agents and accept telemetry)
=== hooks git --help ===
Commands called by git hooks. These delegate to the current strategy.

Usage:
  entire hooks git [command]

Available Commands:
  commit-msg         Handle commit-msg git hook
  post-commit        Handle post-commit git hook
  post-rewrite       Handle post-rewrite git hook
  pre-push           Handle pre-push git hook
  prepare-commit-msg Handle prepare-commit-msg git hook

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for git

Use "entire hooks git [command] --help" for more information about a command.

content.text:
I now understand the complete pipeline. Here's the mechanism in `manual-commit` mode:

1. **`prepare-commit-msg`** → injects an `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer into your commit message when an agent session is active
2. **`commit-msg`** → strips that trailer if you added no real content (lets empty commits abort)
3. **`post-commit`** → if the trailer survived, condenses the session into a checkpoint tied to that commit
4. **`pre-push`** → ships the session logs/checkpoints to the console on `git push`

So your setup is **complete and correct** — login, enable, hooks (cleanly chained behind trunk), and an active session all check out. You have 0 checkpoints simply because **no commit has been made during an agent session since you enabled Entire.** Let me check whether the last commit is already a latent checkpoint waiting to be pushed.

input.command: cd /Users/elvis/Documents/ai/gtm-sdk; echo "=== ahead/behind origin ==="; git rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...HEAD 2>&1; echo "=== HEAD commit full message (trailer check) ==="; git log -1 --format='%B' HEAD; echo "=== any Entire-Checkpoint trailer in recent commits? ==="; git log -5 --format='%H %s' | cat; git log -10 --format='%B' | grep -i "Entire-Checkpoint" || echo "(no Entire-Checkpoint trailer in last 10 commits)"
input.description: Check if main is ahead of origin and whether any commit carries the checkpoint trailer

content.text:
Here's the situation — and it's good news: your Entire setup is **fully correct**. Login ✓, enabled ✓, hooks cleanly chained behind trunk ✓, this session is being captured ✓. You have **0 checkpoints** for one simple reason: no commit has been made *during* an agent session since you enabled Entire (the last commit `4fff502` predates the session, so it never got the `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer).

To produce your first checkpoint in the console, the pipeline needs **one real commit made now (trailer auto-injected) + a push** (the `pre-push` hook ships it to the console). Two wrinkles I need your call on:

- The only unpushed commit is on **`main`**, which is protected — I don't push it without your OK.
- A checkpoint needs *real* content (an empty commit gets its trailer stripped), so a demo means creating a small tracked change.

questions.question: How do you want to generate your first checkpoint to the console?

options.label: Throwaway demo branch
options.description: I branch off main (agent/entire-checkpoint-demo), make one tiny tracked change, commit (trailer auto-injected → checkpoint), run roborev, push the branch → checkpoint lands in the console. main stays untouched; branch deleted after.
options.preview:
git checkout -b agent/entire-checkpoint-demo
# add small tracked file (e.g. .entire/NOTES.md)
git commit -m "chore: entire checkpoint smoke test"
#   → prepare-commit-msg injects Entire-Checkpoint trailer
#   → post-commit condenses session → checkpoint
git roborev review --wait
git push -u origin agent/entire-checkpoint-demo
#   → pre-push ships checkpoint to console

options.label: Commit + push on main
options.description: Most direct: I make a small change, commit on main, run roborev, and push main (which also carries the already-unpushed .entire setup commit). Pushes a protected branch — needs your explicit OK.
options.preview:
git commit -m "..."   # on main
git roborev review --wait
git push origin main
#   pushes 4fff502 (setup) + new checkpoint commit

options.label: No demo — verify only
options.description: Don't create an artificial commit. I confirm the setup is correct and your first checkpoint appears automatically the next time you commit during a session and push a branch. Nothing pushed now.
options.preview:
# setup already verified correct
# next real commit during a session + push
# → checkpoint appears in console automatically
